raw_text:
EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
March 2026 actuals came in at £20,928 against a budget of £15,465, a net overspend of +£5,463 (+35.3%), driven almost entirely by a single deal pulled into the month ahead of schedule. The Meridian Group contract, originally targeted for April close, was accelerated at the customer's request and recognised £5,003 in Sales in March, with a corresponding £750 uplift in Purchases for deployment materials. The remaining variances across operating lines are individually insignificant.

LINE COMMENTARY

Sales | +£5,003 (+83.4%) | Budget: £6,000 | Actual: £11,003
The entire favourable variance is attributable to the Meridian Group deal (CRM ref: crm-opp-2026-MER-001), which was pulled forward 22 days from a 15 April close date to 24 March at the customer's request to align with their financial year-end. March revenue recognition comprises £2,000 (first-month recurring) and £3,003 (accelerated annual element), per the revenue recognition memo countersigned by the CFO on 25 March; this reconciles exactly to the £5,003 variance. As this represents a timing pull-forward rather than new pipeline, April recurring run-rate is expected to normalise accordingly — though the forward schedule requires confirmation.
Sources
- CRM - 2024-11-05T26:15:00Z - crm-sales-broad - crm-opp-2026-MER-001: Meridian Group — Stage: Closed Won. Close date moved from 15 April to 24 March. ARR: £4,800. One-time onboarding: £1,200. Pulled forward by 22 days.
- Gmail - 2024-11-05T18:15:00Z - gmail-sales-broad - gmail-2026-03-24-meridian: RE: Meridian Group — Contract execution. Signed MSA received from Meridian legal on 24 March. Revenue recognition memo attached. CFO countersigned 25 March.
- Slack - 2024-11-05T12:15:00Z - slack-sales-broad - sales-slack-2026-03-24: @channel Q1 close update — Meridian contract signed today, ahead of April target. Deal pulled forward at customer's request to beat their FY end. Total contract value £5,100 (one-time plus first month recurring).
- CRM - 2024-11-05T26:45:00Z - crm-sales-narrow - crm-opp-2026-MER-001: Revenue split — recurring £800/mo + one-time £1,200. Total March recognition: £2,000 first month + £3,003 accelerated annual. See revenue recognition memo.

Purchases | +£750 (+150.0%) | Budget: £500 | Actual: £1,250
The overspend is directly linked to the Meridian onboarding: PO-2026-0312 for £1,250 of deployment materials was raised on 26 March and approved by the Operations Director, matching the actual exactly. This is corroborated by a COGS reconciliation email noting the variance was expected given in-month revenue outperformance. The purchase is consistent with budgeted cost of onboarding but the onboarding itself was not budgeted in March.
Sources
- Slack - 2024-11-09T12:15:00Z - slack-purchases-broad - ops-slack-2026-03-26: Stock order for Meridian onboarding raised — £750 materials for initial deployment. Standard procurement channel, PO attached.
- Gmail - 2024-11-09T18:15:00Z - gmail-purchases-broad - gmail-2026-03-26-cogs: COGS reconciliation for March — direct materials for Meridian onboarding posted in month. Variance is expected given revenue outperformance.
- Gmail - 2024-11-09T18:45:00Z - gmail-purchases-narrow - gmail-2026-03-26-po: Purchase order PO-2026-0312 approved by Operations Director — £1,250 for deployment materials.

INSIGNIFICANT VARIANCES
The following lines are below the significance threshold and require no individual commentary:
- Advertising & Marketing: +£45 (+0.6%)
- Audit & Accountancy fees: -£3 (-6.1%)
- Bank Fees: -£30 (-100.0%)
- Cleaning: -£100 (-100.0%)
- Entertainment - 0%: -£50 (-100.0%)
- Entertainment - 100% business: +£18 (+0.0%)
- General Expenses: +£124 (+103.6%)
- Light Power Heating: +£13 (+14.9%)
- Motor Vehicle Expenses: -£6 (-4.8%)
- Postage Freight Courier: -£50 (-100.0%)
- Printing Stationery: +£46 (+227.9%)
- Rent: -£46 (-4.4%)
- Repairs Maintenance: -£100 (-100.0%)
- Subscriptions: £0 (+0.0%)
- Telephone Internet: -£2 (-4.6%)
- Travel National: -£150 (-100.0%)
